sourceRefs.summary: Fig. 5 maps to SETD1B requirement for broad H3K4me3 establishment.
Fig. 5 legend and result text report Setd1b cKO loss of broad H3K4me3 and differential H3K27ac effects.

inputRefs.summary: S1 ChIP-seq metadata include Setd1bKO H3K4me3 RS4/RS8, H3K27ac RS4, Pol II, TAF3, and input sample labels.
GSE242515 is public and should contain generated ChIP-seq/RNA-seq data, but no GEO files were materialized in this pass.

codeRefs.summary: Parsed S1 sample metadata and counted Setd1b-labeled local sample rows.

parameters.inputScope: published Supplementary Table S1 metadata only
parameters.countingRule: case-insensitive Setd1b label scan in local workbook rows

outputFigureRefs.summary: S1 confirms discoverable perturbation samples but does not provide cKO domain calls or signal matrices needed to reproduce Fig. 5 panels.

statisticsRefs.summary: Local S1 parse found 14 Setd1b-labeled ChIP-seq rows and 8 Setd1b-labeled RNA-seq rows.

stdoutRefs.summary: Local parse stdout includes Setd1b sample counts from S1.

stderrRefs.summary: No stderr from bounded local supplement parse.

limitations: S1 is metadata only; it does not contain Setd1b cKO broad-domain calls, H3K4me3/H3K27ac signal matrices, or the IF quantification required for Fig. 5 reproduction.
No processed GSE242515 BED/bigWig files were downloaded, and no raw ChIP-seq pipeline was run.
Stage purity and developmental delay cannot be separated from direct SETD1B effects with local supplement metadata alone.
diagnostics: Do not treat the PDF's Setd1b cKO statements as reproduced evidence.
The correct current state is data-discovered but computation-insufficient.
